title: Marketing OS Blueprint - & YouTube
notes:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1b9tsd4ckGiGgut98ZjrQiD7d0S6s1-IL/view?usp=drivesdk

https://youtu.be/cHM9QrHURpE?is=OWHMkyPi8RVswzt0

Marketing OS Architecture Blueprint
The 4-Layer System That Tracks All Your Marketing Data Automatically
How to use: This is the exact architecture behind the Marketing OS shown in the video. Use it as a blueprint to build your own version,
starting with just one platform and one data source.
1 Data Sources
Your platforms (YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, email) connected to a data connector that pulls metrics automatically. Zero logins, zero
CSV exports, zero manual work. Set it up once, it runs every morning.
Tools: Windsor.ai, Supermetrics, Make.com, Zapier, or any API connector
▼
2 Storage
Structured files that everything else reads from. Nothing fancy. JSON, YAML, or even a Google Sheet. The point is that your data
lives in one organized place, not scattered across five dashboards.
Tools: JSON/YAML files, Google Sheets, Notion database, Airtable
▼
3 Intelligence (The Dashboard)
Where raw data becomes insight. Growth curves, week-over-week trends, competitor feeds, content velocity, demographics, per-
video performance. This is the hero layer. Open it every morning and know exactly where you stand.
Tools: Custom dashboard (built with Claude Code), Looker Studio, Metabase, even a well-structured Google Sheet
▼
4 Action
Your content pipeline and automation layer. Track every video from Idea to Published. AI skills handle research, scripts, titles,
thumbnails, and repurposing. Insights from Layer 3 drive what you create in Layer 4.
Tools: Airtable, Notion, Claude Code skills, n8n, Make.com
The 6 Metrics That Actually Matter
Views per video
Is this topic/title getting clicks?
Average View Duration %
Is this content holding attention?
Subscribers gained per video
Is this video growing the channel?
Week-over-week view trend
Is the channel growing or declining?
CTR (click-through rate)
Is the thumbnail/title combo working?
Content velocity
Are you publishing enough, consistently?
The test: if a number moved significantly this week, would you change a decision? If not, stop tracking it.
Where to Start
1 Pick one platform.
Don't connect everything on day one. Start with YouTube or wherever your main audience lives.
2 Find your data connector.
Most platforms have an API or a connector tool. If you can get data into a spreadsheet automatically, Layer 1 is done.
3 Build Layer 3 before Layer 2.
A simple dashboard (even a Google Sheet with charts) is more valuable than a perfect storage system nobody looks at.
4 "Good enough to use daily" is the target.
Not perfect. Not comprehensive. Just useful enough that you open it every morning. Then stop building and start creating.
Starter Prompt
Paste this into Claude Code to build your first dashboard:
Build me a simple marketing analytics dashboard.
I post on [YouTube / TikTok / Instagram].
Pull my data from [data source, e.g. a CSV export or Google Sheet].
Show me: views per video, week-over-week trends, and a chart of subscriber growth.
Keep it simple. One HTML file. I want to open it every morning and know where I stand.

title: CHANGE UPDATE MECHANISM ON DASHBOARDS
notes:
So last night before I went to bed, the website that is hosted in Netlify was paused and went down because we consumed all the credits. It appears that they have a credit system that every deploy uses up 20 credits to free account gets 300 credits so basically after 15 deployments and the deployment is every time every 30 minutes I guess you’re sending information over it’s one deployment so at the moment based on every 30 minutes for all 24 hours that’s 48 deployments that’s 900 almost 1000 credits so I have to upgrade to the nine dollar account which provides 1000 credits a month. We’re consuming 1000 credits a day and the $20 month account is 3000 credits a month which would only be three days worth the other thing I noticed is that we’re updating 24 hours a day which is not needed, but we need to really discuss changing the mechanism because I only want to get the updated information when I may fetch it when I click a reload it gets it so the question is when I click reload can it go out to you and get it? I mean during the day there’s gotta be a way to minimize this credit usage so I want you to think through and look at the documentation and notify to understand how it works, how the credit system works. Because at the moment this doesn’t make sense now or in the future. There’s gotta be a better way. Is there a way to host this on GitHub and we don’t have to pay for these deployment or if it’s on Nelly file like we have it now but we just deploy a few times a day and there’s a way to have an updated dashboard that we keep up because every deployment to notify is 20 credits in the nine dollar plan 1000 credits the $20 plan is 3000 credits. There’s gotta be a better way in a free way to host this so we don’t have to pay every time it’s updated.  Can you think through the options go research figure out if we can host something on our computer or somewhere online- if we’re gonna move from Netlify? What are the options?
